text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller, manageable tasks. Prioritization is equally important—decide which tasks are most urgent and focus on those first. Scope management means understanding what you can realistically achieve within the available time. If too many tasks pile up, don’t be afraid to say no or adjust your goals. Finally, communication is key. If you think you might miss a deadline, inform the relevant people early and suggest a revised timeline. This shows responsibility and helps maintain trust.
